symbols.name: Event
symbols.doc: Event defines event data for the local event bus.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Data is the event payload.

fields.name: Module
fields.doc: Module is the topic publisher module name.

fields.name: OccuredAt
fields.doc: OccuredAt is the event occurrence time.

fields.name: Topic
fields.doc: Topic is the event topic.

symbols.name: EventHandler
symbols.doc: EventHandler defines the event handler function signature.

fields.name: Args
fields.doc: Args is the command-line arguments passed to the launcher, for example ["--headless", "--no-sandbox"].

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Env is a list of environment variables in the format KEY=VALUE, for example ["PATH=/usr/bin", "DEBUG=1"].

fields.name: Id
fields.doc: Id is the unique identifier of the GUI application.

fields.name: LaunchUri
fields.doc:
LaunchUri is the URI used to open the GUI window, for example weixin://launchapplet?appid=xxxx.
If provided, the application will be launched and this URI will be opened.

fields.name: Launcher
fields.doc: Launcher is the launcher command or executable path, for example C:\Program Files\App\app.exe or /usr/bin/app.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the GUI application name.

fields.name: ProcessName
fields.doc: ProcessName is the process name associated with the GUI window, used for window detection and management.

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc:
Timeout is the timeout in milliseconds for launching the application.
If 0, no timeout limit is applied.

fields.name: WaitTime
fields.doc:
WaitTime is the wait time in milliseconds after launching the application before returning.
Useful for allowing the GUI to fully initialize.

fields.name: WindowHeight
fields.doc: WindowHeight is the preferred window height in pixels. If 0, uses default.

fields.name: WindowWidth
fields.doc: WindowWidth is the preferred window width in pixels. If 0, uses default.

fields.name: WorkDir
fields.doc:
WorkDir is the working directory for the launched application.
If empty, defaults to the directory of the launcher executable.

symbols.name: MobileApplication
symbols.doc: MobileApplication defines mobile application configuration for Android/iOS devices.

fields.name: Action
fields.doc:
Action is the Intent action, for example android.intent.action.MAIN or android.intent.action.VIEW.
If empty, defaults to android.intent.action.MAIN.

fields.name: Activity
fields.doc:
Activity is the Activity class name to launch, for example .MainActivity.
Used with Package to form the component for adb command: adb shell am start -n package/activity.

fields.name: Extras
fields.doc:
Extras is a JSON object containing Intent extras (key-value pairs).
For example: {"key1": "value1", "key2": "value2"}.

fields.name: Flags
fields.doc:
Flags is the Intent flags as a comma-separated list, for example "FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK,FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP".
These are passed to adb with --flags parameter.

fields.name: Id
fields.doc: Id is the unique identifier of the mobile application.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the mobile application name.

fields.name: Package
fields.doc: Package is the application package name, for example com.example.app.

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc:
Timeout is the timeout in milliseconds for starting the application.
If 0, no timeout limit is applied.

fields.name: WaitTime
fields.doc:
WaitTime is the wait time in milliseconds after launching the application before returning.
Useful for allowing the app to fully initialize.

fields.name: Author
fields.doc: Author is the script author.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description describes script functionality and usage.

fields.name: Devices
fields.doc: Devices is the list of applicable devices, for example ["windows", "mac", "ubuntu"].

fields.name: Email
fields.doc: Email is the contact address.

fields.name: EngineVersion
fields.doc: EngineVersion is the required engine version.

fields.name: License
fields.doc: License is the license, based on the LICENSE file or declaration.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the module name.

fields.name: Package
fields.doc: Package is the script package name. Only scripts under this package can be imported and called.

fields.name: Tags
fields.doc: Tags is a list of script topic tags, for example ["web automation", "data extraction"].

fields.name: UpdateTime
fields.doc: UpdateTime is the last update time (last commit time), formatted as RFC3339.

fields.name: Version
fields.doc: Version is the module version.

fields.name: Website
fields.doc: Website is the official website of the script.

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain is the source domain of the module, such as github.com.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the project name, such as utils.

fields.name: Owner
fields.doc: Owner is the module owner, such as a GitHub username.

methods.name: ModulePath
methods.doc:
ModulePath generates a module path based on the given root directory.
If the name part contains "/", it is joined as nested directories.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the full module name string in the format
"domain/owner/name" or "owner/name/v2".
If the name part contains "/", it is also handled correctly,
for example "domain/owner/name/subname@version".

symbols.name: ModuleRuntime
symbols.doc:
ModuleRuntime defines script runtime context capabilities.
This interface provides access to windows, system services, cache, variables,
and the local event bus.

methods.name: BooleanVariable
methods.doc: BooleanVariable gets a boolean script variable value.

methods.name: BrokerInfo
methods.doc: BrokerInfo gets the broker information.

methods.name: BrowserWindow
methods.doc: BrowserWindow gets a browser window by window ID.

methods.name: CurrentModule
methods.doc: CurrentModule gets the module name of the currently executing script.

methods.name: FilePathVariable
methods.doc: FilePathVariable gets a file path script variable value.

methods.name: FileSystem
methods.doc: FileSystem gets the local filesystem interface.

methods.name: FloatVariable
methods.doc: FloatVariable gets a float script variable value.

methods.name: GetCacheData
methods.doc: GetCacheData gets data temporarily stored at runtime.

methods.name: HttpClient
methods.doc: HttpClient gets the HTTP client interface.

methods.name: IntegerVariable
methods.doc: IntegerVariable gets an integer script variable value.

methods.name: InvokeWorker
methods.doc:
InvokeWorker calls an exposed method from another local IPC worker.
The `module` identifies the target worker, which corresponds to the module name of the worker.
Note: The `module` does not include the version number, as the system determines the version based on the `go.mod` file.
The `method` is the name of the exposed method to call.
The `dto` (data transfer object) can be either a primitive type or a struct annotated with JSON tags.
The return value is always a JSON string representing the result of the method call.
If the invocation fails, an error is returned.
methods.signature: InvokeWorker(module string, method string, dto any) (string, error)

methods.name: JsonVariable
methods.doc: JsonVariable gets a JSON script variable value.

methods.name: Logger
methods.doc: Logger gets the script logger.

methods.name: MainModule
methods.doc: MainModule gets the module name of the entry point.

methods.name: MobileWindow
methods.doc: MobileWindow gets a mobile window by window ID.

methods.name: OcrWorker
methods.doc: OcrWorker gets the OCR capability worker interface.

methods.name: OsGuiWindow
methods.doc: OsGuiWindow gets an OS GUI window by window ID.

methods.name: Publish
methods.doc:
Publish publishes an event to the local event bus.
The topic is the name of the event topic to publish, and the data is the event payload.
It returns an error if the publication fails.

methods.name: SetCacheData
methods.doc: SetCacheData temporarily stores data at runtime.

methods.name: Storage
methods.doc: Storage gets the project local storage interface.

methods.name: StringVariable
methods.doc: StringVariable gets a string script variable value.

methods.name: Subscribe
methods.doc:
Subscribe subscribes to an exposed event from another local IPC worker/yscript or the current worker/yscript.
The module identifies the target worker to subscribe to, and the topic is the name of the event topic to subscribe to.
The handler is the function that will be called when the event is published.
It returns a Subscriber object representing the subscription or an error if the subscription fails.
methods.signature: Subscribe(module string, topic string, handler EventHandler) (Subscriber, error)

methods.name: Unsubscribe
methods.doc:
Unsubscribe cancels a subscription to an exposed event from another local IPC worker/yscript or the current worker/yscript.
The subscriber is the Subscriber object representing the subscription to cancel.

methods.name: VisionWorker
methods.doc: VisionWorker gets the vision capability worker interface.

symbols.name: ParseModuleSpec
symbols.doc:
ParseModuleSpec parses a module spec string.
Accepted formats are "owner/name[/subname...]" or "domain/owner/name[/subname...]",
and an optional version suffix can be appended as "moduleName@version".
The domain part is optional and must be a valid domain name when present.
The owner part is required and must be a single word (no '/').
The project name part is required and may contain '/'.
It returns a ModuleName struct, version string, and an error if parsing fails.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description is the permission description.

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: Path is the path to the file or directory.

fields.name: Permission
fields.doc: Permission is the access permission, such as r(read)/w(write)/d(delete)/x(execute)/a(all).

fields.name: DefaultValue
fields.doc: DefaultValue is the default value of the variable.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description is the variable description.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the variable name.

fields.name: Required
fields.doc: Required indicates whether the variable is required.

fields.name: Save
fields.doc: Save indicates whether to save the variable to project storage.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type is the variable type, such as string, integer, boolean, or json.

symbols.name: Subscriber
symbols.doc:
Subscriber defines event subscriber information.
It is used to identify subscription source, topic, and status.

methods.name: GetModule
methods.doc: GetModule gets the module name that owns the topic.

methods.name: GetTopic
methods.doc: GetTopic gets the subscribed event topic.

methods.name: IsActive
methods.doc: IsActive indicates whether the subscription is still active.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description is the permission description.

fields.name: Permission
fields.doc: Permission is the access permission, such as g(get)/p(post)/d(download)/u(upload)/a(all).

fields.name: Url
fields.doc: Url is the URL pattern, supporting wildcard (*) and script variable ${var_name}.

symbols.name: VariableDataType
symbols.doc: VariableDataType defines data types for script variables.

methods.name: Parse
methods.doc:
Parse parses a string into a value of the corresponding data type.
If parsing fails, it returns the original string.

methods.name: ToString
methods.doc:
ToString converts any value into the string representation of the corresponding type.
If conversion fails, it returns fmt.Sprintf("%v", value).

fields.name: Args
fields.doc: Args is a list of additional browser command-line arguments, for example ["--proxy-server=localhost:8080", "--disable-plugins"].

fields.name: BrowserType
fields.doc:
BrowserType is the browser type to use, for example "chrome", "firefox", "edge", "safari".
If empty, defaults to system default browser.

fields.name: Id
fields.doc: Id is the unique identifier of the browser application.

fields.name: Incognito
fields.doc: Incognito indicates whether to open the browser in private/incognito mode.

fields.name: LoadTimeout
fields.doc:
LoadTimeout is the timeout in milliseconds for page loading.
If 0, no timeout limit is applied.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is the browser application name.

fields.name: Url
fields.doc: Url is the browser application URL to open, for example https://www.google.com.

fields.name: UserDataDir
fields.doc:
UserDataDir is the custom user data directory for the browser profile.
If empty, uses the default profile directory.

fields.name: WaitTime
fields.doc:
WaitTime is the wait time in milliseconds after opening the URL before returning.
Useful for allowing the page to fully load.

fields.name: WindowHeight
fields.doc: WindowHeight is the preferred window height in pixels. If 0, uses default.

fields.name: WindowWidth
fields.doc: WindowWidth is the preferred window width in pixels. If 0, uses default.

symbols.name: YScript
symbols.doc: YScript defines the top-level script configuration structure.

fields.name: GuiApps
fields.doc: GuiApps is the list of GUI applications to operate.

fields.name: MobileApps
fields.doc: MobileApps is the list of mobile applications to operate.

fields.name: Module
fields.doc: Module is the basic script module information.

fields.name: PathPermissions
fields.doc: PathPermissions is the list of filesystem permissions.

fields.name: ScriptDependencies
fields.doc: ScriptDependencies is the list of script module names (moduleName@version) imported by this script project.

fields.name: UrlPermissions
fields.doc: UrlPermissions is the list of network URL permissions.

fields.name: Variables
fields.doc:
Variables is the list of global script variable definitions.
${script_root} && ${data_root} are reserved variables representing the script root directory and data directory, which can be used in variable default values and permission definitions.

fields.name: Version
fields.doc: Version is the module version.

fields.name: WebApps
fields.doc: WebApps is the list of web applications to operate.

fields.name: WorkerDependencies
fields.doc: WorkerDependencies is the list of IPC service module names (moduleName@version) depended by this script project.

methods.name: ApplyVariables
methods.doc: ApplyVariables applies variable values to the script configuration.
